title: 2x4 Captain's Bed with Flip-Down Storage
summary: A low platform bed with two hinged flip-down panels on the side, opening into storage bins underneath — built almost entirely from 2x4s.
description:
This captain's bed keeps its parts count low by building the whole platform from 2x4 frames, then hanging hinged panels off the sides that flip down to reveal storage underneath instead of building traditional pull-out drawers. That's a real simplification: no drawer slides, no drawer boxes to build square, just a hinged face and open storage bins or totes behind it.

Square frames matter more here than on most beds, because the flip-down faces need to sit flush inside the frame openings to close cleanly — a slightly out-of-square frame shows immediately as an uneven gap around the panel. Mount the door handles higher on the panel than centered; the plan's own build notes that a centered handle gives poor leverage when flipping the panel down from a standing position.

tools.note: Rips plywood or 1x8 stock to the headboard/footboard and drawer-face widths.
Squaring the two main frames is the single most important check in this build.

materials.name: 2x4 board, 8 ft
materials.note: Pine is easier to work with and paint than fir.

materials.name: 1x material or 3/4" plywood, for mattress slats

cutList.note: Add two more if the bed will be exposed and open on both sides.
Space about 4" apart; adjust count to your mattress maker's recommendation.

steps.title: Cut all the parts
steps.body: Cut four frame top and bottom rails at 70-1/2" and six frame verticals at 11-1/2" from the 2x4. Cut two headboard/footboard panels at 36" from the 3/4" plywood. Cut two flip-down drawer faces at 32-3/4" from the 1x12. Cut two mattress slat trim at 72-1/2" from the 1x2. Cut two end slat trim at 33-1/2" and eight mattress slats at 33-1/2" from the 1x4. Allowing about 1/8" for each saw cut, that comes to four 8 ft 2x4, one 6 ft 1x12, two 8 ft 1x2 and five 6 ft 1x4. Square every cut and label each piece as it comes off the saw.
materials: 1x material or 3/4" plywood, for mattress slats

steps.title: Build the two main frames
steps.body: Build two 2x4 frames from the rails and verticals with 2-1/2" to 3" self-tapping screws (a screw type that doesn't need pre-drilling saves real time here), or pocket holes set to 1-1/2". Square is critical — the drawer/flip-down faces need to fit inside these frame openings, and an out-of-square frame will show as an uneven gap once the panels are hung.

steps.title: Build the ends
steps.body: Build the headboard and footboard panels from plywood or stacked 1x boards, and screw them onto the ends of the two main frames with 2" screws and wood glue.

steps.title: Hang the flip-down faces
steps.body: Attach hinges to mount the drawer faces to the frames as flip-down panels. Mount the handles higher on the face than dead-center — a centered handle gives poor leverage when you flip the panel open standing up.

steps.title: Add top trim and finish
steps.body: Stain the 1x2 and 1x4 trim boards before attaching them (much easier than masking off assembled parts), then screw the 1x2s to the top of the bed with 2" screws and glue, overhanging 1/4" on the outside edges. Screw the 1x4 end trim to the ends the same way, matching the 1/4" overhang.

steps.title: Add slats
steps.body: Lay in mattress slats spaced about 4" apart across the frame, resting on the slat trim, and screw down at the ends.
materials: 1x material or 3/4" plywood, for mattress slats

images.alt: Low platform bed with a hinged flip-down storage panel on the side
